refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).

candidate_dps: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency
Batch update mechanism that processes full worksite model periodically within the timeliness constraint
Event-driven update mechanism triggered by detected changes in the worksite environment
Parallel processing architecture that divides the worksite model into segments updated concurrently
Prioritization scheme that updates critical worksite areas first to meet timeliness for operational use
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on processing only changed data incrementally, which logically isolates update processing from unrelated model parts, supporting independence and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that changes in the worksite model can be detected and isolated efficiently
Risk that incremental updates may become complex if changes are highly interdependent
Assumes that the system can meet the p95 ≤ 1.0 s latency target with incremental processing under expected load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining mechanisms for change detection and isolation in the DP to ensure clarity and support modular implementation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core data processing and update mechanisms for the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data updates and sends processed incremental updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication of incremental updates to other system components.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental update mechanism is central to NGHE's data processing; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module uses processed data but does not primarily handle incremental updates; would cause diffusion to NGHE and Teleoperation.
Teleoperation consumes updates but does not process incremental updates; poor containment.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, unrelated to incremental data processing; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental update mechanism logically belongs within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for core data processing and update logic, minimizing latency by processing only changed data internally.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if incremental updates need to be tightly synchronized.
If incremental update logic grows to include cross-module coordination, diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of an incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by minimizing latency. This logical solution concept aligns well with the functional requirement and supports meeting the acceptance criteria of p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if incremental updates need to be tightly synchronized.
If incremental update logic grows to include cross-module coordination, diffusion risk increases.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The incremental update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (8) and low diffusion score (2). Other modules have poor containment and higher diffusion scores, indicating minimal scattering of the DP. Current architectural packaging minimizes diffusion risk, but vigilance is needed if cross-module coordination increases.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring update latency metrics (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) on the NGHE Solution module, which owns the incremental update mechanism and interfaces with telemetry and network link.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate is clearly the NGHE Solution module, which encompasses the core data processing and update mechanisms, providing clear allocation of responsibility for the FR realization.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface complexity and diffusion risk as incremental update logic evolves, especially regarding synchronization with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Consider defining explicit interface contracts and coordination protocols to manage potential diffusion if cross-module interactions increase.
Document latency verification procedures tied to the NGHE Solution module to ensure traceability from FR through DP to IM.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The incremental update mechanism meets the latency requirements and is well contained within the NGHE Solution module with manageable diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, the current level is sufficiently decomposed to allow focused verification and risk monitoring without further sub-FR decomposition at this time.

revise.why: To monitor and manage interface complexity and diffusion risks as the incremental update logic evolves, especially regarding synchronization with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, and to document latency verification procedures for traceability.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism as designed will continue to meet latency requirements under operational conditions.
The NGHE Solution module ownership remains stable and well-defined.
Interface complexity and diffusion risks can be effectively managed through monitoring and explicit interface contracts.
open_risks: Potential increase in interface complexity and diffusion risk if incremental update logic expands to cross-module coordination.
Verification procedures need to be documented and maintained to ensure traceability and compliance with latency requirements.
evidence_needed: Latency performance metrics (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) from NGHE Solution module during operation.
Interface contract definitions and coordination protocols if cross-module interactions increase.
Monitoring reports on diffusion risk and containment scores over time.
